constraints: Consider a grid of size N×MN \times MN×M, where each cell contains one of the characters `R`, `G`, or `B`.
You are asked to count the number of valid grids such that there exists at least one tuple (i,j,d)(i, j, d)(i,j,d), where:
(i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is a starting cell in the grid;
ddd is a direction with value up, down, left, or right;
moving two steps from (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) in direction ddd, and concatenating the values at respective cells (in order), forms the string `RGB`.
Since the answer can be huge, you should print it modulo a given prime number PPP.
The first and only line of the input consists of three space-separated integers NNN, MMM, and PPP — the number of rows of the grid, the number of columns, and the prime modulus, respectively.
Output a single line containing a single integer — the number of valid grids modulo PPP.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The two possible grids are:

{{R, G, B}} : Choose the tuple (1,1,(1, 1, (1,1, right))).
{{B, G, R}} : Choose the tuple (1,3,(1, 3, (1,3, left))).

Explanation:
There are no grids of size 2×12\times 12×1 that contain the string RGB.
